name: pair
title: pair(x, y) -> pair
description:
PRIMITIVE
Makes a pair whose head (first component) is x and whose tail (second component) is y.

name: is_pair
title: is_pair(x) -> boolean
description:
PRIMITIVE
Returns True if x is a pair and False otherwise.

name: head
title: head(p) -> value
description:
PRIMITIVE
Returns head (first component) of given pair p.

name: tail
title: tail(p) -> value
description:
PRIMITIVE
Returns tail (second component) of given pair p.

name: is_none
title: is_none(x) -> boolean
description:
PRIMITIVE
Returns True if x is the empty linked list None, and False otherwise.

name: is_linked_list
title: is_linked_list(xs) -> boolean
description:
PRIMITIVE
Returns True if xs is a linked list as defined in the textbook, and
False otherwise.

name: linked_list
title: linked_list(<CODE>value1,) -> linked_list
description:
PRIMITIVE
Given n values, returns a linked list of length n. The elements of
the linked list are the given values in the given order.

name: draw_data
title: draw_data(<CODE>value1,) -> None
description:
PRIMITIVE
Visualizes the arguments in a separate drawing area in the Source
Academy using box-and-pointer diagrams.

name: equal
title: equal(xs, ys) -> boolean
description: Returns True if both have the same structure (pairs) and identical values at corresponding leaf positions.

name: length_linked_list
title: length_linked_list(xs) -> int
description: Returns the length of the linked list xs.

name: map_linked_list
title: map_linked_list(f, xs) -> linked_list
description: Returns a linked list that results from linked list xs by element-wise application of unary function f.

name: build_linked_list
title: build_linked_list(fun, n) -> linked_list
description: Makes a linked list with n elements by applying the unary function fun to the numbers 0 to n - 1.

name: for_each_linked_list
title: for_each_linked_list(fun, xs) -> None
description: Applies the unary function fun to every element of the linked list xs.

name: linked_list_to_string
title: linked_list_to_string(xs) -> string
description: Returns a string that represents linked list xs using the text-based box-and-pointer notation.

name: reverse_linked_list
title: reverse_linked_list(xs) -> linked_list
description: Returns linked list xs in reverse order.

name: append_linked_list
title: append_linked_list(xs, ys) -> linked_list
description: Returns a linked list that results from appending the linked list ys to the linked list xs.

name: member_linked_list
title: member_linked_list(v, xs) -> linked_list
description: Returns first postfix sub-linked list whose head is identical to v (using ==). Returns None if the element does not occur in the linked list.

name: remove_linked_list
title: remove_linked_list(v, xs) -> linked_list
description: Returns a linked list that results from xs by removing the first item from xs that is identical (==) to v.

name: remove_all_linked_list
title: remove_all_linked_list(v, xs) -> linked_list
description: Returns a linked list that results from xs by removing all items from xs that are identical (==) to v.

name: enum_linked_list
title: enum_linked_list(start, end) -> linked_list
description: Makes a linked list with elements from start to end (inclusive).

name: ref_linked_list
title: ref_linked_list(xs, n) -> value
description: Returns the element of linked list xs at position n (0-indexed).

name: accumulate_linked_list
title: accumulate_linked_list(f, initial, xs) -> value
description: Applies binary function f to the elements of xs from right-to-left order.

name: filter_linked_list
title: filter_linked_list(pred, xs) -> linked_list
description: Returns a linked list that contains only those elements for which the one-argument function pred returns True.
